newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Today's +7.24% move appears flow-driven: continuation of the 8-inch/power-semiconductor rerating cycle (LS Securities target hike citing accelerating 8-inch foundry supply shortage; DS Securities TP raised ~88% to KRW 210,000 in May; Sangsangin TP KRW 208,000), sustained institutional net buying (6 consecutive days), and the PCIM 2026 power-semi expo in Nuremberg starting today (June 9-11) where DB HiTek is showcasing SiC/GaN and BCDMOS processes - marketing optics, not contracts. Underlying fundamentals (Q1 revenue +26% YoY, OP +21%, AI/datacenter PMIC demand, ASP hikes from Q2) were already known in May.

sources.label: BusinessPost - LS Securities raises DB HiTek target price on 8-inch foundry supply shortage
ZDNet Korea - DB HiTek to exhibit SiC/GaN at PCIM 2026 (June 9-11, Nuremberg)
Nate News - AI and power semis drive DB HiTek boom-cycle narrative
Hankyung - DB HiTek 52-week high on heavy institutional net buying

verdicts.rankRationale: Another +7.2% flow-driven leg (now +131% YTD) on sell-side target hikes and PCIM optics, with zero new contracts or guidance since June 6, further compresses residual alpha for a legacy 8-inch specialty foundry whose link to the advanced-node AI tollgate (CPO/HBM/packaging) was already the weak point flagged in yesterday's down verdict.
verdicts.bull: 8-inch foundry supply genuinely tightening as peers migrate to 12-inch; ASP hike cycle from Q2 2026 plus AI/datacenter PMIC demand gives high fixed-cost operating leverage, and SiC/GaN mass production in 2027 adds a second act.
verdicts.neutral: Q1 results (revenue +26%, OP +21%) confirm the power-semi turnaround, but that evidence is a month old and consensus has fully repriced it via near-doubled price targets; the stock now trades on momentum, below its May high.
verdicts.bear: A trailing-edge PMIC/DDI foundry up 131% YTD on a cyclical utilization recovery is the classic post-rerating chase; if 8-inch tightness eases or institutional flows reverse, the multiple has no advanced-node AI moat to fall back on.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 results evidencing the ASP-hike margin leverage, or concrete SiC/GaN customer qualification wins out of PCIM 2026.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding unwind: momentum/institutional flows reverse on any power-semi cycle wobble, with valuation already discounting the 2027 SiC/GaN ramp.
